newsletters.subject: Nvidia's AI Factory Boom Hits $81.6B
newsletters.summary: General tech/business newsletter. Covers NVIDIA earnings ($81.6B), a quantum chip research note, SpaceX IPO, ChatGPT PowerPoint beta, Qwen3.7 Max, AI policy, and Demis Hassabis interview. Mix of AI-relevant and non-AI topics. The Qwen3.7 Max section is the most directly wiki-relevant. All 20 links are beehiiv tracking URLs (unresolvable to targets without following each redirect).

newsletters.subject: [AINews] All Model Labs are now Agent Labs
newsletters.summary: Premier AI newsletter covering the biggest shift in the AI industry: model labs pivoting to become agent labs. Key signals: Greg Brockman declares 'the model alone is no longer the product', AI21 shuts model team to pivot to agents, DeepSeek forms first Harness team. Comprehensive coverage of coding agents (Codex, Claude Devs), model pricing disruption (DeepSeek V4-Pro permanent 75% discount), MCP stateless RC, sandboxes, research (VPO, agent distillation, LT2). This is the single most important AI newsletter of the week for the wiki's domain.

classified_articles.title_guess: Substack internal links (post, like, comment, share, app)
classified_articles.reason: Substack engagement/app links — not article content.

classified_articles.resolved_title: Agent Labs: Welcome to GPT Wrapper Summer
classified_articles.reason: Core thesis article defining the 'Agent Labs' category. Framework for understanding industry structure. Foundational concept for wiki.

classified_articles.resolved_title: [AINews] All Model Labs are now Agent Labs (full issue)
classified_articles.reason: Full AINews issue. Core content: Model Labs → Agent Labs thesis + Greg Brockman statement + AI21/DeepSeek pivots + coding agent updates + DeepSeek V4-Pro pricing + MCP RC + research. MULTIPLE wiki-relevant sections.
MCP protocol evolution to stateless. Major infrastructure shift for agent tooling. First-class MCP Apps and Tasks, auth hardening.

classified_articles.resolved_title: X post: Core Automation + Joanne Jang on product vs model
classified_articles.reason: Industry discourse on model vs product. Joanne Jang: 'the model should be invisible... alpha is in making models feel natural.' Part of the Model→Agent narrative.
DeepSeek V4-Pro permanent pricing: $0.435/M input, $0.87/M output. 3-19× cheaper than competitors. 'Intelligence too cheap to meter.' Major model economics signal.
Codex major update: Appshots, /goal improvements, remote computer use while locked, annotation mode, plugin sharing, analytics. User reports abandoning IDEs.
Research: entire agentic workflow can be distilled into model weights, running at ~100× lower cost while preserving quality. Significant for agent economics.

classified_articles.resolved_title: Greg Brockman X post: 'the model alone is no longer the product'
classified_articles.reason: Direct quote from Greg Brockman (OpenAI president), 627 replies, 6.5K likes. Marks a strategic pivot at OpenAI ahead of IPO. Foundational signal.

classified_articles.resolved_title: Gemini 3.5 Flash analysis / Qwen3.7-Max / Cursor Composer 2.5
classified_articles.reason: Model performance roundup: Gemini 3.5 Flash improvements + builder skepticism, Qwen3.7-Max analysis, Cursor Composer 2.5 cost advantages. Multiple model entity updates needed.
RL research: VPO uses vector-valued rewards to prevent collapse. LT2 linear-time looped transformer. Research to track for techniques pages.
swyx meta/editorial on content quality and scaling Latent Space. Not AI technical knowledge.
